Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee; Bring Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; And Set Bond For Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-11-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard concurrently:
PCN-15-299351 Conservatorship of Joan Spencer.
PTR-19-303280 In the Matter of the Joan Spencer 2009 Revocable Trust.

Appearances:
Caroline Hinshaw, court appointed counsel for Conservatee/settlor of trust.
Andrew Schultz for and with Andrea Leung, Conservator/proposed successor trustee

The Court noted that the Trust matter will be discussed before the Conservatorship matter and inquired if it is Ms. Leung's intention to move venue to San Mateo County. Mr. Schultz responded affirmatively and presented a proposed order to include additional language. The Court inquired about the change of assets. Mr. Schultz stated the only assets are the cash as set forth in the supplemental declaration, that the property in Little Rock, AR that he previously spoke of was part of a 2013 quiet title action that belonged to Ms. Spencer's mother and the title was never transferred, that the house is no longer an asset of the mother's estate and she doesn't have a right to it. The Court noted the power of attorney provided 2 children would serve and if one could not serve the other one can serve, whether Ms. Spencer has capacity to enter into power of attorney now, and what steps can be taken to revive the power of attorney. Mr. Schultz stated there is a probate code section that allows power of attorney, that since the court ordered the power of attorney revoked, the court can issue an order to revive that power of attorney. Ms. Hinshaw stated the court can modify the order that revoked the power of attorney. Mr. Schultz stated that they did consult an attorney in Tennessee and the attorney suggested to find a professional fiduciary. The Court inquired if there was any reason to hold these orders until March 19th for the next hearing on termination of conservatorship. Mr. Schultz stated he does not think so.

The Petition to Transfer Conservatorship Assets to Trust by Substituted Judgment (PCN-15-299351) and The Petition to Appoint Successor Trustee, etc. (PTR-19-303280), are Granted (subject to review of written orders).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee; Bring Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; And Set Bond For Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-22-2020 Continued To Mar-04-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with Andrea Leung.
Caroline Hinshaw Court appointed Counsel for Joan Spencer.

The Court stated that the order of things is a little bit confusing and noted that substituted judgment is usually done first and then termination of the conservatorship. Ms. Leung stated that Joan moved to Tennessee in 2018. Mr. Schultz stated that they initiated the conservatorship because of very active APS issues, that one Child was financially abusing the Mother, that another Child misappropriated two pensions of the Father, that one Child has disabilities and had always lived with the Mother, that there were capacity issues and that there is a lot of distrust between the four Children. Mr. Schultz also stated Sheila (Daughter) was the stable one and removed herself, that the Conservatee had no estate documents, that they asked for estate documents, but none were provided, that they told Sheila that she needed to get a Fiduciary in Tennessee and that they were told that she wanted Andrea. In addition, Mr. Schultz stated that the Power of Attorney names Sheila and Darryl, and that Sheila is not willing to work with Darryl, but she is also not willing to step aside for him. The Court stated that it does not think the trust should be here in San Francisco and that the Settlor's intent was for the Kids to serve with not alternatives. Mr. Schultz stated that the Conservatee is under the supervision of the Court, that the trust is under the supervision of the Court, that they are asking for transfer of the assets (cash from sale of the property) from the conservatorship to the trust and assuming the that the finances can be handled through the trust and the Power of Attorney is reinstated, there is no need for a conservatorship in Tennessee. In addition, Mr. Schultz stated that they have asked the Family, but no one is sure if property in Arkansas exist anymore, and read language ("shall be governed by the laws of the State of California") from the trust document. Ms. Hinshaw stated that Ms. Leung would be the best person. The Court stated that venue should be in San Mateo County. The Court wants a declaration regarding exploration of a Fiduciary in Tennessee and Sheila's position as to why she wants Andrea Leung as the Fiduciary. The Petition to Appoint Successor Trustee, etc. is continued to March 4, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to file a separate petition for substituted judgment which is to be given a hearing date of March 4, 2020. The Petition to Terminate Conservatorship of the Person and Estate in case number 299351 is continued from January 23, 2020 to March 19, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
